message:
fix(window): per-function partition/sort for multiple window specs (#85)

When a SELECT had multiple window functions with different OVER clauses
(e.g., SUM(x) OVER (PARTITION BY region) and RANK() OVER (ORDER BY score)),
all functions were evaluated against the first function's PARTITION BY and
ORDER BY. This caused RANK() to compute within-partition ranks instead of
global ranks.

Root cause: window evaluation sorted rows once using win_partition_by[0]
and win_order_by[0], then built a single shared partition set.

Fix: compute per-function sorted indices and partitions independently.
Each window function now gets its own sort order and partition grouping
from its own OVER clause. Results are scattered back to raw row indices,
then output rows are sorted by the first window spec (matching SQLite's
implicit output ordering).

Also removed stale debug eprintln in execute_statement SELECT path.

Added 19 new conformance oracle tests: aggregate DISTINCT, deeply nested
derived tables, IN subquery patterns, complex HAVING, ORDER BY mixed
direction, trigger WHEN clause, JOIN USING, GROUP BY NULLs, FK CASCADE,
LIMIT/OFFSET edges, multi-column PK, INSERT expressions, mixed storage
types, typeof, last_insert_rowid/changes.

126 oracle ext tests pass. 2049+ core tests, 0 new failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
